UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile within the Campus Hub application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user profile.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

profilePicture.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

registrationDate.description: Date and time the user registered.

IntranetPost.title: IntranetPost
IntranetPost.description: Represents a post on the intranet portal.

id.description: Unique identifier for the intranet post.

title.description: The title of the intranet post.

content.description: The main content of the intranet post.

publicationDate.description: Date and time the intranet post was published.

summary.description: AI-generated summary of the intranet post.

VirtualClassroomCourse.title: VirtualClassroomCourse
VirtualClassroomCourse.description: Represents a course within the virtual classroom.

CourseAssignment.title: CourseAssignment
CourseAssignment.description: Represents an assignment within a course.

dueDate.description: The date and time the assignment is due.

AssignmentSubmission.title: AssignmentSubmission
AssignmentSubmission.description: Represents a student's submission for an assignment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the assignment submission.

submissionDate.description: Date and time the assignment was submitted.

grade.description: The grade received for the assignment submission.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Uses path-based ownership for private data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores intranet posts. No specific authorization denormalization is needed as listing can be controlled at the collection level. Each post is self-contained.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier for the intranet post.

definition.description: Stores virtual classroom course information. Ownership is determined by the instructorId.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the virtual classroom course.

definition.description: Stores course assignments. Includes denormalized instructorId from VirtualClassroomCourse for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the virtual classroom course.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the course assignment.

definition.description: Stores assignment submissions. Includes denormalized instructorId from VirtualClassroomCourse and studentId for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the virtual classroom course.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the course assignment.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the assignment submission.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, following the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization. For example, access control lists or roles associated with a `VirtualClassroomCourse` are denormalized into the `CourseAssignment` documents, avoiding costly and insecure `get()` calls in security rules. Structural Segregation is used to maintain a homogeneous security posture, such as storing `IntranetPost` under `/intranet_posts` instead of mixing them with other data types. Access Modeling employs consistent patterns like path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/profile`) and membership maps for collaborative data, ensuring a standardized approach to authorization. Data Clarity is achieved through explicit state modeling using dedicated status fields where appropriate and predictable schema.
